name: git-worktree-safety
description: Host-neutral read-only diagnosis for missing, stale, or broken Git worktrees and convenience symlinks, with ref/reflog retention classification and POSIX-only exact-pointer repair.

interface.shortDescription: Recover stale worktree pointers without changing Git state.
interface.longDescription: A host-neutral Git audit plugin for diagnosing vanished worktree paths and broken convenience symlinks. It proves live branch identity, classifies ref, reflog-only, object-only, unknown, and missing commit retention, and validates one exact clean registered replacement. Exact compare-and-swap-style symlink repair is available only on POSIX runtimes with proven directory-relative and atomic-replace support; other hosts remain read-only.
capabilities: NUL-safe registered worktree inventory and exact branch binding
Branch-reachable, reflog-only, object-only, and missing commit classification
Clean symbolic replacement proof bound to the live branch ref
Broken convenience-symlink inspection with raw paths omitted from JSON
POSIX-only fingerprint and raw-target guarded exact symlink repair
Post-repair proof that relevant Git state remained unchanged
interface.defaultPrompt: Audit this missing Git worktree or broken convenience symlink and determine whether an exact pointer-only repair is safe.
